# YOUR CODE HERE
import sys

def main():
    import sys
    input = sys.stdin.read
    data = input().split()
    
    N = int(data[0])
    D = int(data[1])
    P = int(data[2])
    F = list(map(int, data[3:]))
    
    # Sort F in descending order to maximize savings
    F.sort(reverse=True)
    
    total_cost = sum(F)  # Start with the total regular fare
    savings = 0
    
    # Use as many passes as possible on the most expensive days
    for i in range(0, N, D):
        # Take the next D most expensive days
        batch = F[i:i+D]
        batch_sum = sum(batch)
        # Compare cost of pass vs sum of F_i for these days
        if P < batch_sum:
            total_cost -= batch_sum - P
        else:
            # No savings, do not use the pass
            pass
    
    print(total_cost)

if __name__ == "__main__":
    main()
